dbBikes.blurb: The best-selling fat e-bike in the US. Owners swap the heavy Kenda Juggernauts for street tires and ditch the stock saddle within the first month.
Rad's commuter workhorse. Saddle, grips, and pedals are the classic first-week takeoffs.
Moped-style utility bike. The bench pad and mechanical brakes are the most common swaps.
America's best-selling e-bike, period. Upgrading the mechanical brakes to hydraulic is the #1 mod, so clean stock brake sets are everywhere.
Budget cargo hauler. Contact points get replaced fast once owners start carrying kids.
Fat-tire flagship with torque sensor. Fat tires come off for commuter rubber constantly.
Torque-sensing commuter. Saddle and suspension fork are the usual first upgrades.
Upright cruiser. Cruiser saddle and swept-back grips get personalized more than any other part.
Value commuter favorite. Mozo fork and stock contact points are frequent takeoffs.
Comfort commuter. Mechanical brakes and the plush-but-wide saddle are the usual swaps.
Juiced shut down in 2024, so working 52V batteries, displays, and controllers from parted-out bikes are in serious demand.
Dealer-sold Bosch bike. Motor and battery stay put — Bontrager contact points and tires are what come off.
Premium commuter. Owners fine-tune saddle, grips, and tires; the takeoffs are barely used.
Dutch touring bike with premium stock parts — Ultimate takeoffs sell fast on the used shelf.

The bike everyone comes to gawk at. Owners upgrade everything — suspension, controller, seat — so takeoff supply is deep and moves slower than commuter parts. Credit runs lean; clean parts still find homes.
Sur-Ron's gearbox-driven rival. Same story: huge parts culture, everything gets swapped, takeoffs pile up. We price accordingly.
The style icon. Seats, bars, and tires get personalized constantly — stock takeoffs are plentiful and mostly cosmetic-swap pulls in great shape.
The third name in the e-moto trio. Suspension and controller swaps dominate; stock parts show up here constantly.

dbParts.stock_part: 52V 19.2Ah pack — high demand since Juiced closed

dbCatalog.name: Stock 48V 10-14Ah battery pack
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: The range-anxiety cure: bigger 52V pack

dbCatalog.name: Stock 500-750W hub motor (wheel included)
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: Hub-to-mid-drive is the big-league swap

dbCatalog.name: Stock controller
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: More amps, smoother throttle mapping

dbCatalog.name: Stock LCD display
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: App-programmable and half the size

dbCatalog.name: Stock mechanical disc brake set
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: The #1 upgrade on every cable-brake bike

dbCatalog.name: Stock hydraulic brake set (MT200 class)
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: MT200s work; MT5e's stop a loaded bike

dbCatalog.name: Stock tire pair (street or fat)
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: Street rubber transforms fat-tire commuters

dbCatalog.name: Stock saddle
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: Usually paired with a suspension seatpost

dbCatalog.name: Stock grips / cockpit parts
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: Wing grips fix numb hands on hour-plus rides

dbCatalog.name: Stock rear rack or fender set
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: Standardized mounts beat proprietary racks

dbCatalog.name: Stock 7/8-speed drivetrain (derailleur + shifter + cassette)
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: LinkGlide is built for motor torque

dbCatalog.name: Stock 36V battery pack
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: Bosch riders usually add a second OEM pack

dbCatalog.name: Stock pedals
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: Pinned alloy platforms, first week

dbCatalog.name: Stock light set (headlight + taillight)
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: High-lumen units wired to the pack

dbCatalog.name: Stock coil suspension fork
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: Air fork upgrade, usually 100-120mm

dbCatalog.name: Stock e-moto battery (60V class)
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: 72V aftermarket packs for more top end

dbCatalog.name: Stock e-moto controller
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: Aftermarket 72V FOC controller — the amp chase

dbCatalog.name: Stock e-moto display + throttle
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: Aftermarket dash + quick-turn throttle

dbCatalog.name: Stock e-moto fork + rear shock set
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: DNM / Fastace upgrade, usually first month

dbCatalog.name: Stock e-moto seat
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: Tall comfort seat or grippy MX cover

dbCatalog.name: Stock e-moto knobby tire pair
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: Street moto rubber or aggressive MX knobbies

dbCatalog.name: Stock sprocket + chain (e-moto)
dbCatalog.upgrade_note: Taller gearing for street, shorter for trail

dbProducts.name: Cycle Satiator Programmable Charger
dbProducts.description: Set an 80% charge profile for daily commuting and a 100% profile for long weekends. Doubles the calendar life of most packs.

dbProducts.name: 52V 17.5Ah Extended Range Pack
dbProducts.description: Samsung 50E cells in a hard-case downtube pack. Roughly 40% more usable range than the stock 48V 13Ah packs shipped on most commuter e-bikes, with the same rail mount.

dbProducts.name: 35A Sine-Wave Controller
dbProducts.description: Silent sine-wave replacement for the buzzy square-wave controller most hub-drive bikes ship with. Direct plug for KT displays.

dbProducts.name: Rad Power Stock 22A Controller
dbProducts.description: OEM controller pulled during a 35A upgrade. A cheap way to get a dead bike back on the road.

dbProducts.name: Light Bee Stock Rear Shock
dbProducts.description: The stock Light Bee coil-over, pulled when the previous owner went to a DNM. Full travel, no leaks, hardware included. If you are building a spare bike or just want a known-good backup, this is the cheap way in.
dbProducts.condition_note: Takeoff at roughly 100 miles. Cosmetic scuffs on the spring, damper is tight, no seepage.

dbProducts.name: Light Bee Stock Controller
dbProducts.description: Stock 60V Sur-Ron controller from an amp-chasing upgrade pull. Every connector intact, no heat discoloration. The smart money keeps one of these in the parts bin — aftermarket controllers die, this one just works.
dbProducts.condition_note: Pulled working from a running bike. Case scratches only, wiring uncut with factory connectors.

dbProducts.name: RX Stock Bench Seat
dbProducts.description: The classic Super73 diamond-stitch bench, swapped for a custom cover two weeks after purchase like they always are. Rails and mounting hardware included. This is the part everyone breaks and nobody can find.
dbProducts.condition_note: Two weeks of use. Minor corner wear on the piping, foam and rails are perfect.

dbProducts.name: BBSHD 1000W Mid-Drive Kit
dbProducts.description: The default answer for hill country. 160Nm at the crank, fits a standard 68-73mm bottom bracket, and takes the stock chainring you already have.

dbProducts.name: Photon Mid-Drive
dbProducts.description: Compact 250W-rated mid-drive that peaks near 750W. Torque-sensing, quiet, and legal-limit friendly out of the box.

dbProducts.name: EggRider V2 Bluetooth Display
dbProducts.description: Tiny display, huge control. Change speed limits, current limits, and PAS curves from your phone. Two rider profiles.

dbProducts.name: CUES LinkGlide 11-50T Cassette
dbProducts.description: LinkGlide steel is rated for three times the wear life of standard cassettes. Built specifically for mid-drive torque.

dbProducts.name: E-Bike Rated 10sp Chain
dbProducts.description: e10 EPT chain with hardened pins for mid-drive loads and anti-rust coating for all-weather commuters.

dbProducts.name: MT5e 4-Piston Brake Set
dbProducts.description: Front and rear 4-piston hydraulics with cut-off sensors. The single most important upgrade on any 60lb+ e-bike.

dbProducts.name: Disc Wave 203mm E-Rated Rotor
dbProducts.description: Thicker 2.0mm e-bike rated rotor that resists fade on long descents with a loaded bike.

dbProducts.name: Marathon E-Plus 26x2.15
dbProducts.description: The flat-proof standard for e-bikes. Smart DualGuard belt, ECE-R75 rated for fast e-bikes, reflective sidewall.

dbProducts.name: Hookworm 20x1.95 Fat-Resist
dbProducts.description: Favorite swap for 20" folding and cargo bikes. Smooth-rolling, huge air volume, wears like iron.

dbProducts.name: NCX Suspension Seatpost
dbProducts.description: Parallelogram suspension post that erases chatter better than most front forks. 50mm travel, tool-free preload.

dbProducts.name: GP1 Evo Ergonomic Grips
dbProducts.description: Wing-shaped grips that fix numb hands on upright commuters. Clamp-on, no glue.

dbProducts.name: Explorer Rack + MTX QuickTrack
dbProducts.description: Disc-compatible rear rack with the MTX quick-release track. 26kg rated, fits fat and standard frames.

dbProducts.name: Bosch Purion Display (Takeoff)
dbProducts.description: Removed at 40 miles when the owner swapped to a Kiox 300. Zero scratches, includes bar clamp.

dbProducts.name: Kenda Krusade 26x4 Pair (Takeoff)
dbProducts.description: Stock fat-bike takeoffs with visible tread wear but plenty of life for a season of street riding. Sold as a pair.
dbProducts.condition_note: Tread ~70%. Small sidewall scuff on one tire.

dbProducts.name: Selle Royal Stock Comfort Saddle
dbProducts.description: The saddle everyone replaces on day one, in case yours went the other direction and you want it back.

dbProducts.name: Rad Power 48V 14Ah Battery (Takeoff)
dbProducts.description: Pulled from a 2025 RadCity 5 with 210 miles when the owner upgraded to a 52V pack. Capacity tested at 97% of rated.
dbProducts.condition_note: Capacity tested 97%. Light rail scuffs. 90-day warranty.

dbProducts.name: MT200 Hydraulic Brake Set (Takeoff)
dbProducts.description: The classic first-week takeoff: pulled when the owner went to 4-piston Maguras. Bled, pads at 90%, ready to bolt on.
dbProducts.condition_note: Pads at ~90%. Freshly bled. Rotors not included.
